name: Chocolate Malt Cake
recipeInstructions:
Cake: Preheat oven to 375°F. Lightly butter two 9-inch cake pans with 2 tablespoons butter each, then dust with 1/4 cup flour and shake off excess.
Mix together the remaining 2 1/2 cups of flour with baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a separate bowl. Beat egg whites until stiff peaks form. In another bowl, beat the remaining sugar with 3/4 cup butter until light. Add egg yolks one at a time. Stir in melted chocolate and beer. Gradually beat in the flour mixture alternately with the wet ingredients. Fold in egg whites using a rubber spatula.
Scrape half the batter into each cake pan. Bake for 30-35 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Let cool completely.
Frosting: Soften chocolate chips and butter in a double boiler. Remove from heat and beat chocolate and butter until smooth. Add beer and milk one tablespoon at a time until the mixture is soft and shiny. Frost cake layers. Any beer will work, especially those with strong flavors such as porter, stout, ale, or lager. If you prefer not to use beer, substitute cider or non-alcoholic beer.
